description: Public confidence in the MMR vaccine plummeted in the UK following Andrew Wakefield's 1998 Lancet paper and subsequent media coverage. This scenario tracks the decline and eventual stabilization of pro-vaccine sentiment between 2001 and 2003 as health officials fought to counter misinformation.

key_events.description: High-profile media reports amplify link between MMR and autism.
Measles cases rise sharply; NHS launches emergency 'Catch-up' campaign.
Lancet issues partial retraction/criticism of Wakefield's study.
Long-term data confirms vaccine safety; public trust begins slow recovery.

calibration_notes: Tests model sensitivity to health misinformation campaigns and recovery periods.
